Code Summary: This project implements a comprehensive MLOps pipeline for predicting longitudinal cognitive decline using deep learning models trained on the OASIS-2 dataset. The code features modular architectures including baseline LSTM models for tabular data and hybrid CNN+LSTM models that combine clinical data with 3D T1-weighted MRI scans through late fusion. The pipeline emphasizes reproducibility through structured notebooks, experiment tracking with Weights & Biases, and includes uncertainty quantification and model interpretability techniques.
Data Types: - 3D T1-weighted MRI neuroimaging data - Clinical/tabular data including Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR) scores - Longitudinal time-series data from OASIS-2 dataset - Future plans include PET imaging and genetic data from ADNI dataset
Tooling: - Deep Learning: PyTorch (implied from CNN+LSTM models) - Experiment Tracking: Weights & Biases (W&B) - Neuroimaging Processing: FSL, ANTs/ANTsPyNet, TemplateFlow, NIfTI - Model Interpretability: SHAP, Integrated Gradients, Permutation Feature Importance - Development: Jupyter Notebooks, Git, Python - Uncertainty Quantification: MC Dropout - Data Processing: Standard Python data science stack (pandas, numpy implied) - Configuration Management: JSON-based configuration system
